businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and Physical AI platform spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla is transitioning from EV manufacturing to an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform. FY2025 revenue $94.8B (-2.9% YoY), net income $3.79B (-46.5% YoY). Monday close $395.56 (+1.1%). Terafab AI chip fab confirmed for 3/21 (Saturday) launch with vertical integration of logic, memory, and advanced packaging. Today (3/17) the US government confirmed Tesla's $4.3B LFP battery factory deal with LG Energy Solution for Megapack 3 cells.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)
keyProducts: Cybercab - Autonomous robotaxi (April production start planned)
Megapack 3 - Next-gen utility battery (5 MWh, LG LFP cells coming 2027)
Optimus - Humanoid robot (Fremont factory conversion underway)
Terafab - AI chip fabrication facility (3/21 launch, logic+memory+packaging)
Macrohard - Tesla-xAI joint enterprise AI system

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 13.6x the S&P 500 average (27x) and 24x the traditional auto sector average (15x). Extreme premium reflecting AI, robotics, and energy expectations

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: 9x the S&P 500 Forward P/E (21x). Far above EV peers BYD (18x) and Rivian (unprofitable). Based on FY2026 EPS consensus of $2.56

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $94.8B declined. Delivery drops and price cuts weighed. Q4 revenue $24.9B (-3.1% YoY)

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Margin compression from pricing competition. Net margin 4.00%. BYD price war intensifying

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: Solid cash generation. Total cash $44B on hand

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Excellent balance sheet. Among the lowest in large-cap tech. Current ratio 2.16

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Low return on equity vs. large-cap tech peers. Margin recovery is key

keyMetrics.name: RSI
keyMetrics.interpretation: Neutral to bearish. Attempting a bounce on Terafab hype and LG battery deal, but momentum remains weak

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP Battery Factory Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Reuters (3/17): US government officially confirmed Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP prismatic battery manufacturing facility in Lansing, Michigan. Production starts 2027 for Megapack 3 cells. Larger 2.8-liter cells deliver 5 MWh per unit (up 28% from 3.9 MWh). Secures domestic battery supply chain.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab 3/21 Launch D-4: Logic+Memory+Packaging Vertical Integration
growthDrivers.description: Teslarati (3/16): Terafab goes beyond AI chips, integrating logic processing, memory storage, and advanced packaging in a single 2nm-class facility. Unprecedented vertical integration. $20B+ investment.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Line Designed for Hundreds per Week, April Start
growthDrivers.description: Teslarati (3/12): Cybercab line designed for hundreds of vehicles per week. Targeting 2M units annually. Fully autonomous with no steering wheel or pedals. Regulatory hurdles remain the key risk.

growthDrivers.title: Macrohard (Digital Optimus): Tesla-xAI Joint Enterprise AI
growthDrivers.description: Simply Wall St (3/16): Musk unveiled Macrohard, a Tesla-xAI joint enterprise AI system. Combined with Terafab, it completes the AI hardware+software vertical integration.

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Jan-Feb +35% YoY, Global Recovery Signal
growthDrivers.description: Finbold (3/15): China-made Tesla EV sales reached 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. Europe Feb registrations also up 10% YoY.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share. FSD 8.2B miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration are core moats. However, BYD has overtaken global sales and the competition gap is narrowing.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly more training data than any competitor.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab logic+memory), software (FSD), and charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, and Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2025: 2.26M vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging tech

recentNews.headline: US government confirms Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP battery factory deal for Megapack 3
recentNews.significance: Reuters: LFP prismatic cell facility in Lansing, Michigan. Production starts 2027, contract through 2030. Megapack 3 delivers 5 MWh per unit (28% increase). Critical energy supply chain secured

recentNews.headline: Terafab scope expanded: Logic+memory+packaging under one roof. Stock +1.1%
recentNews.significance: Electrek: Zero semiconductor experience attempting world's largest 2nm fab. Success means NVIDIA independence, failure means billions in sunk costs

recentNews.headline: Syrah-Tesla graphite supply dispute extended to June 1 (4th extension)
recentNews.significance: Reuters: Graphite supply contract dispute deadline extended from 3/16 to 6/1. Immediate supply crisis averted but uncertainty persists

recentNews.headline: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds per week. April production target
recentNews.significance: Teslarati: Fully autonomous vehicle with no steering wheel/pedals. Targeting 2M annual units. Regulatory hurdle is the key risk

recentNews.headline: NHTSA deepens FSD investigation: Austin robotaxi 14 traffic violations, railroad crossing incident
recentNews.significance: goodcarbadcar: 2.88M vehicles under investigation since FSD data deadline (3/9). 80 traffic violations. Regulatory risk persists

analystOpinions.notableComment: Consensus $406.84 (+2.9%), majority Hold. New Street Research $600 (high), Wells Fargo $125 (low, Underweight). Simply Wall St fair value $421.61. LG battery deal may lift energy business forecasts.

risks.title: BYD competition accelerating, global EV share reversed
risks.description: BYD 2025 EV sales 2.26M (+28%) vs Tesla 1.64M (-8%). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging. Aggressive expansion in emerging markets.

risks.title: Terafab execution risk: Zero semiconductor experience
risks.description: Electrek (3/16): Tesla has zero chip manufacturing experience attempting the world's largest 2nm fab. Logic+memory+packaging integration that even TSMC and Samsung haven't attempted. Billions at risk if it fails.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD investigation expansion, Cybercab regulatory hurdles
risks.description: 2.88M vehicles under FSD investigation. Austin robotaxi 14 traffic violations, railroad crossing incident. Cybercab requires regulatory approval before US sales.

risks.title: Extreme valuation at 366x P/E
risks.description: TTM P/E 366x, Forward P/E 186x, PEG 11.57. Multiple could compress sharply if growth slows or competition intensifies.

risks.title: Insider selling continues + supply chain uncertainty
risks.description: Insiders sold 87,995 shares ($38.31M) over 3 months. Syrah graphite dispute extended to 4th deadline (6/1). Cybercab trademark dispute ongoing.

risks.title: Musk leadership spread + DOGE controversy
risks.description: Simultaneously running Tesla, xAI, SpaceX, X (Twitter), DOGE, and Macrohard. Focus dilution concerns.

buyReasons.title: LG $4.3B Battery Deal + Terafab 3/21 Launch: Energy+AI Dual Expansion
buyReasons.rationale: US-made LFP cells secured for Megapack 3 accelerate energy business growth. Terafab is the first step toward NVIDIA and TSMC independence. Both growth pillars materializing simultaneously.

buyReasons.title: Cybercab April Production + Macrohard AI Business
buyReasons.rationale: First step toward robotaxi commercialization. Macrohard enters enterprise AI market. Revenue diversification beyond automotive.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35%, Energy Business 5 Consecutive Quarters of Record Profit
buyReasons.rationale: China rebound signals global delivery recovery. Energy gross profit hit $1.1B, reducing EV dependence.

buyReasons.title: Optimus Robot + Fremont Factory Conversion
buyReasons.rationale: Model S/X discontinued for Optimus production. Success opens $12T+ robotics TAM.

buyReasons.title: Strong Balance Sheet: $44B Cash
buyReasons.rationale: D/E 0.08, current ratio 2.16. Ample cash to fund Terafab ($20B+), LG battery deal, and other large-scale investments.

overallOpinion: Today the US government confirmed Tesla's $4.3B LFP battery factory deal with LG Energy Solution. US-made cells for Megapack 3 start production in 2027, securing a critical energy supply chain. Terafab launch is D-4 (Saturday 3/21), and Cybercab April production is on track.

However, NHTSA has expanded its FSD investigation to 2.88M vehicles, and insider selling totaled $38.31M over 3 months. Electrek directly criticized Tesla's Terafab plans given zero semiconductor experience.

At 366x P/E, below both the 50-day ($420) and 200-day ($426) moving averages, RSI at 40. Consensus target $407 (+2.9%). The LG deal boosts energy expectations, but it's wiser to wait for Saturday's Terafab launch results before making a move.

investmentVerdict.summary: The LG $4.3B battery deal secures energy supply chain, and Terafab+Cybercab+Macrohard provide ample catalysts. But 366x P/E, expanding NHTSA investigation, and persistent insider selling are headwinds. Wait for the 3/21 Terafab launch results before reassessing.

threads.hook: Tesla-LG $4.3B battery deal confirmed, Terafab D-4

$TSLA (Mar 17) $395.56 (+1.1%)

US government confirmed Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP battery factory today. US-made cells for Megapack 3 start production in 2027 at Lansing, Michigan. Each unit now delivers 5 MWh, up 28% from 3.9 MWh. Energy business supply chain puzzle is finally complete.

Terafab Saturday launch D-4. Cybercab April production and Macrohard AI also lined up. But NHTSA is expanding its 2.88M-vehicle FSD probe, and insider selling hit $38M in 3 months.

P/E 366x, below both 50-day and 200-day averages. RSI 40. Target $407 (+2.9%). Wait for Saturday's Terafab results before making a move.

witty.hook: Tesla: batteries, chips, robots, energy, AI. What's next?
witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 17) $395.56 (+1.1%)

LG $4.3B battery factory confirmed. Megapack 3 now 5 MWh, 28% bigger. Saturday brings Terafab launch. April brings Cybercab. Tesla is trying to make everything, and the market gave it +1.1%.

NHTSA is probing 2.88M vehicles, the CFO sold shares, and P/E is 366x. This company is a permanent battleground between bulls and bears.

Target $407 (+2.9%). Saturday we learn if Terafab rewrites semiconductor history or becomes a $20B lesson.

empathy.hook: TSLA holders, good news keeps coming but the stock isn't moving
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 17) $395.56 (+1.1%)

LG $4.3B battery deal, Terafab D-4, Cybercab on track. News keeps pouring in, but the stock sits below the 50-day ($420) and barely moves. That has to be frustrating.

NHTSA probe and insider selling are dragging. But securing the energy supply chain is genuinely significant progress. $44B cash provides a solid buffer.

Saturday is the inflection point. If Terafab succeeds, the entire mood could shift. It's okay to wait for the results. Target $407 (+2.9%).
